doc.id: Potkay and Feng - 2023 - Do stomata optimize turgor-driven growth A new framework for integrating stomata response with whol_appendix.pdf
doc.title: Do stomata optimize turgor-driven growth?: A new framework for integrating stomata response with whole-plant hydraulics and carbon balance (New Phytologist Supporting Information)

model_card.name: S5 Xylem hydraulics
task: compute steady-state xylem water potentials (root collar, stem apex) from transpiration
compute sensitivities dψ/dE needed for stomatal growth optimization
optionally compute leaf water potential for comparison to other stomatal models

assumptions.text: Xylem water potentials throughout the tree are assumed steady-state for a given transpiration rate; this corresponds to negligible hydraulic capacitance (tissue water content in steady-state).

evidence.snippet: Xylem water potentials throughout the tree are assumed in steady-state for a given transpiration rate... hydraulics system with negligible capacitance.

assumptions.text: In steady-state, the total water flux through the tree (aL·E) is conserved through both roots and stem and is modeled by Darcy’s law along normalized coordinates z~ (stem) and d~ (roots).

evidence.snippet: In steady-state, the total amount of water conducted through the tree, aL∙E, is conserved through both the roots and the stem, and this flux is given by Darcy’s law,

assumptions.text: Conductive fractions for stem and soil-root systems are exponential sigmoidal (logistic) functions of water potential.

evidence.snippet: The conductive fractions, VCR and VCS, are both functions of water potential and described here by exponential sigmoidal equations,

assumptions.text: Leaf hydraulics are not required for the growth optimization algorithm, but leaf water potential ψL is computed for comparisons to other stomatal models.

evidence.snippet: growth optimization algorithm... does not explicitly require considering xylem transport through leaves... we present the calculations for the leaf water potential, ψL

equations.explanation: Stem (aboveground woody biomass) steady-state Darcy-law relation along normalized axial coordinate.

evidence.snippet: (S5.1)  ∂ψ/∂z̃ = aL E / (kS VCS(ψ)) + ϖ ρ g H

equations.explanation: Soil-root steady-state Darcy-law relation along normalized depth coordinate.

evidence.snippet: (S5.2)  ∂ψ/∂d̃ = aL E / (kR VCR(ψ)) + ϖ ρ g Z

equations.explanation: Stem conductive fraction (relative to maximum) as a logistic function of water potential.
Soil-root conductive fraction (relative to maximum) as a logistic function of water potential.
Analytic distribution of stem xylem water potential along z~ derived by combining Eq.S5.1 and Eq.S5.3 and integrating.
Root-collar xylem water potential solution from boundary conditions and integrated soil-root system.

evidence.snippet: (S5.6)  ψRc = ψsoil - (χR+aLE)/kR + βR + (1/αR) ln[ ... ]

equations.explanation: Stem-apex xylem water potential solution from boundary conditions and integrated stem system.

evidence.snippet: (S5.7)  ψS = ψRc - (χS+aLE)/kS + βS + (1/αS) ln[ ... ]

equations.explanation: Sensitivity of root-collar water potential to transpiration; obtained by differentiating Eq.S5.6 w.r.t. E.

evidence.snippet: (S5.8)  ∂ψRc/∂E = -aL/kR + aL/[αR(χR+aLE)] [ ... ]

equations.latex: \frac{\partial \psi_S}{\partial E}=\frac{\partial \psi_{Rc}}{\partial E}-\frac{a_L}{k_S}+\frac{a_L}{\alpha_S(\chi_S+a_L E)}\Bigg[\left(\frac{\chi_S}{\chi_S+a_L E}-\alpha_S E\frac{\partial \psi_{Rc}}{\partial E}\right)\exp\left(-\alpha_S(\psi_S-\beta_S)-\alpha_S\frac{\chi_S+a_L E}{k_S}\right)-\left(\frac{\chi_S}{\chi_S+a_L E}+\alpha_S\frac{a_L E}{k_S}-\alpha_S E\frac{\partial \psi_{Rc}}{\partial E}\right)\exp\left(-\alpha_S(\psi_S-\beta_S)\right)\Bigg]
equations.explanation: Sensitivity of stem-apex water potential to transpiration; obtained by differentiating Eq.S5.7 w.r.t. E.

evidence.snippet: (S5.9)  ∂ψS/∂E = ∂ψRc/∂E - aL/kS + aL/[αS(χS+aLE)] [ ... ]

equations.explanation: Leaf conductive fraction (relative to maximum) for computing leaf water potential in comparisons.
Leaf water potential (gravitational terms removed) analogous to Eq.S5.6–S5.7 for leaves.

evidence.snippet: (S5.11)  ψL = ψS - E/kL + βL + (1/αL) ln[ ... ]

equations.explanation: Sensitivity of leaf water potential to transpiration; leaf-analog of Eq.S5.8–S5.9 with gravity removed.

variables.name: water potential along stem/root domains
variables.definition: Water potential along either \tilde{z} (stem) or \tilde{d} (roots).

evidence.snippet: \psi is the water potential [MPa] along the domains of either z̃ or d̃

variables.name: soil water potential
variables.definition: Soil water potential used as a boundary condition for the soil-root system.

evidence.snippet: soil water potentials, ψsoil [MPa]

variables.name: xylem water potential at the root collar
variables.definition: Root-collar xylem water potential; solved such that fluxes are conserved between organs.

evidence.snippet: xylem water potentials at the root collar... ψRc

variables.name: xylem water potential at the stem apex
variables.definition: Stem-apex xylem water potential; solved such that fluxes are conserved between organs.

evidence.snippet: xylem water potentials... the stem apex, ψRc and ψS, respectively [MPa]

variables.name: leaf water potential
variables.definition: Leaf water potential used for comparison to other stomatal models (not used by the growth model).

evidence.snippet: we present the calculations for the leaf water potential, ψL [MPa]

variables.name: normalized axial distance from stem apex
variables.definition: Unitless axial distance from the stem-apex normalized by H: \tilde{z}=z/H; increasing downward.

evidence.snippet: z̃ is the unitless axial distance from the stem-apex normalized by H (z̃ = z/H...); note that increasing values... are downward

variables.name: normalized distance from root collar (depth)
variables.definition: Unitless distance from the root collar normalized by Z: \tilde{d}=d/Z; increasing downward.

evidence.snippet: d̃ is the unitless distance from the root collar normalized by Z (d̃ = d/Z...); note that increasing values... are downward

variables.name: tree height
variables.definition: Tree height used in the stem gravitational term and normalization of \tilde{z}.

evidence.snippet: where H is tree height [m]

variables.name: rooting depth
variables.definition: Rooting depth used in the soil-root gravitational term and normalization of \tilde{d}.

evidence.snippet: Z is the rooting depth [m]

variables.name: maximum stem conductance
variables.definition: Maximum conductance of the stem system.

evidence.snippet: kS and kR are the maximum conductances [mol H2O∙s-1∙MPa-1] of the stem and soil-root systems

variables.name: maximum soil-root conductance
variables.definition: Maximum conductance of the soil-root system.

evidence.snippet: kS and kR are the maximum conductances [mol H2O∙s-1∙MPa-1] of the stem and soil-root systems

variables.name: maximum leaf conductance per leaf area
variables.definition: Maximum leaf conductance per unit leaf area used in leaf water potential equation.

evidence.snippet: kL is the maximum leaf conductance per leaf area [mol H2O∙m-2∙s-1∙MPa-1]

variables.name: stem conductive fraction
variables.definition: Conductive fraction of the stem system relative to maximum; logistic in \psi.

evidence.snippet: VCS(ψ) = 1/(1+exp[-αS(ψ-βS)])

variables.name: soil-root conductive fraction
variables.definition: Conductive fraction of the soil-root system relative to maximum; logistic in \psi.

evidence.snippet: VCR(ψ) = 1/(1+exp[-αR(ψ-βR)])

variables.name: leaf conductive fraction
variables.definition: Leaf conductive fraction (relative to maximum) used for ψL calculation; logistic in \psi.

evidence.snippet: VCL(ψ) = 1/(1+exp[-αL(ψ-βL)])

variables.name: stem vulnerability curve shape parameter
variables.definition: Logistic (exponential sigmoidal) shape parameter for stem conductive fraction.

evidence.snippet: αS and αR are shape parameters [MPa-1]

variables.name: stem water potential at 50% conductance loss
variables.definition: Potential at which stems have lost half of their conductance.

evidence.snippet: βS and βR are the potentials at which stems and roots have lost half of their conductance [MPa]

variables.name: root vulnerability curve shape parameter
variables.definition: Logistic (exponential sigmoidal) shape parameter for root conductive fraction.

evidence.snippet: αS and αR are shape parameters [MPa-1]

variables.name: root water potential at 50% conductance loss
variables.definition: Potential at which roots have lost half of their conductance.

evidence.snippet: βS and βR are the potentials at which stems and roots have lost half of their conductance [MPa]

variables.name: leaf vulnerability curve shape parameter
variables.definition: Logistic (exponential sigmoidal) shape parameter for leaf conductive fraction.

evidence.snippet: αL is a shape parameter [MPa-1]

variables.name: leaf water potential at 50% conductance loss
variables.definition: Potential at which a leaf has lost half of its conductance.

evidence.snippet: βL is the potential at which a leaf has lost half of its conductance [MPa]

variables.name: Pa-to-MPa conversion factor
variables.definition: Factor converting from Pa to MPa (given as 10^-6 MPa·Pa^-1).

evidence.snippet: ϖ is a factor that converts from Pa to MPa (ϖ = 10-6 MPa∙Pa-1)

variables.name: density of water
variables.definition: Density of water used in gravitational term.

evidence.snippet: ρ is the density of water [kg∙m-3]

variables.name: acceleration due to gravity
variables.definition: Acceleration due to gravity used in gravitational potential term.

evidence.snippet: g is the acceleration due to gravity [m∙s-2]

variables.name: stem gravitational shorthand
variables.definition: Shorthand for \varpi·\rho·g·H·kS.

evidence.snippet: χS is shorthand for ϖ∙ρ∙g∙H∙kS and has units of [mol H2O∙s-1]

variables.name: root gravitational shorthand
variables.definition: Shorthand for \varpi·\rho·g·Z·kR.

evidence.snippet: χR is shorthand for ϖ∙ρ∙g∙Z∙kR and has units of [mol H2O∙s-1]

variables.name: canopy conductance (inverse form referenced)
variables.definition: Referenced as kc = -∂E/∂ψL.

evidence.snippet: inverse of the canopy conductance (kc = -∂E/∂ψL)

variables.name: transpiration rate (as used in this section)
variables.definition: Transpiration rate used to define total flux aL·E and to compute ψ potentials and sensitivities.

evidence.snippet: total amount of water conducted through the tree, aL∙E

variables.name: leaf area (as used in this section)
variables.definition: Scaling factor converting E to total tree flux aL·E.

evidence.snippet: total amount of water conducted through the tree, aL∙E
H is tree height [m]
Z is the rooting depth [m]
kS and kR are the maximum conductances [mol H2O∙s-1∙MPa-1]

kL is the maximum leaf conductance per leaf area [mol H2O∙m-2∙s-1∙MPa-1]
αS and αR are shape parameters [MPa-1]
βS and βR are the potentials at which stems and roots have lost half of their conductance [MPa]

steps: Compute conductive fractions V_{C,S}(\psi) and V_{C,R}(\psi) from Eq.S5.3–S5.4 (logistic vulnerability curves).
Solve for \psi_{Rc} using Eq.S5.6 given \psi_{soil} and E (and parameters k_R, \alpha_R, \beta_R, \chi_R).
Solve for \psi_S using Eq.S5.7 given \psi_{Rc} and E (and parameters k_S, \alpha_S, \beta_S, \chi_S).
Compute sensitivities \partial\psi_{Rc}/\partial E and \partial\psi_S/\partial E using Eq.S5.8–S5.9.
If comparing to other stomatal models, compute leaf conductive fraction V_{C,L}(\psi) (Eq.S5.10), leaf water potential \psi_L (Eq.S5.11), and \partial\psi_L/\partial E (Eq.S5.12).

evidence.snippet: we require solutions for... ∂ψRc/∂E and ∂ψS/∂E... defined by differentiating Eq. S5.6&7 with respect to E

limitations.text: Boundary conditions as written include an apparent inconsistency for \psi(\tilde{z}=1) mapping to both \psi_{Rc} and \psi_S; treat boundary statement as potentially errant unless clarified elsewhere.

evidence.snippet: note that ψ(d̃ = 1) = ψsoil, ψ(z̃ = 1) = ψ(d̃ = 0) = ψRc, and ψ(z̃ = 1) = ψS

implementation.model_scope: Steady-state xylem hydraulics for roots and stem with vulnerability curves; analytic solutions for \psi_{Rc}, \psi_S, and their sensitivities to transpiration. Optional leaf \psi_L module for model comparisons.

evidence.snippet: soil water potentials, ψsoil [MPa], and xylem water potentials... ψRc and ψS
∂ψRc/∂E and ∂ψS/∂E... defined by differentiating Eq. S5.6&7 with respect to E
we derive solutions for xylem water potentials at the root collar and the stem apex
ψ(d̃ = 1) = ψsoil, ψ(z̃ = 1) = ψ(d̃ = 0) = ψRc, and ψ(z̃ = 1) = ψS

unresolved_assets.label: Boundary conditions for \tilde{z} endpoints
unresolved_assets.issue: Boundary statement assigns \psi(\tilde{z}=1) to both \psi_{Rc} and \psi_S; clarify correct endpoint mapping for \psi_S (e.g., whether \tilde{z}=0 is intended).

evidence.snippet: ψ(z̃ = 1) = ψ(d̃ = 0) = ψRc, and ψ(z̃ = 1) = ψS

open_questions.question: What is the correct boundary condition for \psi_S in terms of \tilde{z} (is it \psi(\tilde{z}=0)=\psi_S rather than \psi(\tilde{z}=1)=\psi_S)?
open_questions.why_needed: Implementing Eq.S5.5–S5.7 requires consistent endpoint definitions for stem coordinate \tilde{z}.
open_questions.likely_location: Either a typo here or clarified in Potkay et al. (2021a) SI section S.3 or later sections.

evidence.snippet: ψ(z̃ = 1) = ψ(d̃ = 0) = ψRc, and ψ(z̃ = 1) = ψS

provenance.notes: C005 extracts S5 Xylem hydraulics equations and definitions (pages 32–34). Page 32 includes trailing Eq.S4.19 from prior section; excluded from model_card as out-of-scope for S5.

section_titles: Notes S1 / S1 Whole-tree carbon dynamics
chunk_plan.rationale: Defines NSC storage dynamics and respiration/growth closures used by later optimization.
Derives optimality conditions (η, χw=λ) linking stomata to growth optimization.

section_titles: S3 Leaf temperature and stomatal conductances
chunk_plan.rationale: Defines TL and conductance relationships required for E and An dependencies.

section_titles: S4 Leaf and canopy-level carbon assimilation
chunk_plan.rationale: Defines An and λ=dAn/dE used by χw=λ condition.
Provides analytic solutions for ψRc, ψS and their sensitivities to E used in optimization, plus optional ψL module.

evidence.snippet: S5 Xylem hydraulics
